exercises.prompt: Iron law #1: where does YOUR opinion belong in a synthèse?

options.text: Nowhere — total objectivity
In the conclusion only
In the introduction, briefly

exercises.explanation: The synthèse is strictly objective — opinions belong to the essai, the other DALF task.

exercises.prompt: How should the body of a synthèse be organised?

options.text: Around shared aspects, confronting the documents' ideas
Document 1 summarised, then document 2 summarised
In chronological order of publication

exercises.explanation: Confrontation, not succession: weave the sources around themes.

exercises.prompt: The phrase-outil for the central question.
exercises.template: La {{b1}} commune est la suivante : faut-il encadrer l'IA ?
exercises.explanation: La problématique = the one question all the documents answer.

exercises.template: Contrairement au premier document, le second {{b1}} les risques.
exercises.explanation: Mettre en évidence = to highlight — a core synthèse verb.

exercises.prompt: A sentence from the source text fits your synthèse perfectly. You…

options.text: rephrase the idea entirely in your own words
copy it — it says it best
copy it but add quotation marks

exercises.explanation: Reformulation is mandatory; copying (even quoted) is penalised in a synthèse.

exercises.template: Il {{b1}} de ces documents que le débat reste ouvert.
exercises.explanation: Il ressort de X que… = it emerges from X that…

exercises.prompt: Match each synthèse verb to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: "Both documents deal with remote work."
Listen to this synthèse introduction. What does it announce?
exercises.transcript: Ces deux articles traitent du télétravail ; la problématique commune est la suivante : le travail à distance libère-t-il ou isole-t-il ?

options.text: Two articles on remote work, asking whether it frees or isolates
One article praising office life
The speaker's personal opinion on working from home

exercises.explanation: A model intro: documents presented + problématique announced, zero opinion.

exercises.prompt: Read this mini-synthèse and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: Ces deux documents abordent l'intelligence artificielle à l'école. Le premier, un article de presse, met en évidence les gains de temps pour les enseignants ; le second, un entretien avec une sociologue, soulève au contraire la question de la dépendance des élèves. Il ressort de cette confrontation que l'outil ne dispense pas de la pédagogie : les deux auteurs, chacun à sa manière, appellent à un encadrement réfléchi.
exercises.passageEn: These two documents tackle artificial intelligence at school. The first, a press article, highlights time savings for teachers; the second, an interview with a sociologist, raises on the contrary the question of pupils' dependency. It emerges from this confrontation that the tool does not replace pedagogy: both authors, each in their own way, call for thoughtful regulation.

questions.prompt: Why is this a proper synthèse body (not two summaries)?

options.text: It confronts the two sources around one question
It quotes both documents at length
It gives the writer's verdict on AI

questions.prompt: What shared call do both authors make?

options.text: For thoughtful regulation of the tool
For banning AI from schools
For replacing teachers with AI
Read all → note essential ideas → dégager la problématique → build the plan
Build the plan → read all → copy good sentences → add opinion
Dégager la problématique → read one document → write immediately

exercises.explanation: Understanding precedes structure: read, note, problématique, plan — then write.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Les deux documents traitent de la révolution numérique.

options.text: Both documents deal with the digital revolution.
The shared question is this: does technology free us?
The author raises the question of screen dependency.
a synthesis
a central question / issue
to draw out / identify

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.explanation: « La synthèse confronte les documents. » — a synthesis.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.

pairs.right: to confront / set against each other
a stake / what is at issue

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Les deux documents traitent de la révolution numérique.
exercises.targetEn: Both documents deal with the digital revolution.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: L'auteur soulève la question de la dépendance aux écrans.
exercises.targetEn: The author raises the question of screen dependency.
